channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Google: Hackers used AI to develop zero-day exploit for web admin tool
item.description: Researchers at Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) say that a zero-day exploit targeting a popular open-source web administration tool was likely generated using AI. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
item.description: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar this week: Prevention alone is not enough against modern attacks
item.description: This upcoming webinar explores how organizations need to combine security, backups, and recovery planning to reduce the impact of modern cyberattacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cyber Espionage Group Targets Aviation Firms to Steal Map Data
item.description: The campaign quietly compromises aerospace and drone operators to exfiltrate GIS files, terrain models, and GPS data and gain a clear picture of adversaries' world view....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Your Purple Team Isn't Purple — It's Just Red and Blue in the Same Room
item.description: Defending a network at 2 am looks a lot like this: an analyst copy-pasting a hash from a PDF into a SIEM query. A red team script is being rewritten by hand so the blue team can use it. A patch waitin...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TrickMo Android banker adopts TON blockchain for covert comms
item.description: A new variant of the TrickMo Android banking malware, delivered in campaigns targeting users across Europe, introduces new commands and uses The Open Network (TON) for stealthy command-and-control com...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake OpenAI Privacy Filter Repo Hits #1 on Hugging Face, Draws 244K Downloads
item.description: A malicious Hugging Face repository managed to take a spot in the platform's trending list by impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter open-weight model to deliver a Rust-based information stealer to Win...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers abuse Google ads, Claude.ai chats to push Mac malware
item.description: Attackers are abusing Google Ads and legitimate Claude.ai shared chats in an active malvertising campaign. Users searching for "Claude mac download" may come across sponsored search results that list ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Police shut down reboot of Crimenetwork marketplace, arrest admin
item.description: German authorities have shut down a relaunch version of the criminal marketplace 'Crimenetwork' that generated more than 3.6 million euros, and arrested its operator. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process memory. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: JDownloader site hacked to replace installers with Python RAT malware
item.description: The website for the popular JDownloader download manager was compromised earlier this week to distribute malicious Windows and Linux installers, with the Windows payload found deploying a Python-based...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake OpenAI repository on Hugging Face pushes infostealer malware
item.description: A malicious Hugging Face repository that reached the platform's trending list impersonated OpenAI's "Privacy Filter" project to deliver information-stealing malware to Windows users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service. The li...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
item.description: The edtech company is struggling to wrest control from its hackers. PII belonging to hundreds of millions of people is on the line....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
item.description: Threat hunters have flagged a previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan dubbed TCLBANKER that's capable of targeting 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms. The activity is being tra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NVIDIA confirms GeForce NOW data breach affecting Armenian users
item.description: NVIDIA has confirmed in a statement for BleepingComputer that GeForce NOW user information has been exposed in a data breach. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why More Analysts Won’t Solve Your SOC’s Alert Problem
item.description: Attackers move faster than overwhelmed SOC teams can realistically investigate alerts. Prophet Security breaks down how AI can help analysts investigate alerts faster and focus on real threats. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
item.description: The attack on the Trellix source code repository disclosed last week has been claimed by the RansomHouse threat group, which leaked a small set of images as proof of the intrusion. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA gives feds four days to patch Ivanti flaw exploited as zero-day
item.description: CISA has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their networks against a high-severity vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
item.description: A previously undocumented Linux implant codenamed Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems to establish a silent foothold as well as facilitate a broad range of post-compromise functio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Zara data breach exposed personal information of 197,000 people
item.description: Hackers who gained access to the databases of Spanish fast-fashion retailer Zara stole data belonging to more than 197,000 customers, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: One Missed Threat Per Week: What 25M Alerts Reveal About Low-Severity Risk
item.description: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. This is not just anecdotal, but rather backed by a recent report investi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Former govt contractor convicted for wiping dozens of federal databases
item.description: A 34-year-old Virginia man was found guilty of conspiring to destroy dozens of government databases after getting fired from his job as a federal contractor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux backdoor named PamDOORa that's being advertised on the Rehub Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 by a threat actor called "darkworm." Th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Linux 'Dirty Frag' zero-day gives root on all major distros
item.description: A new Linux zero-day exploit, named Dirty Frag, allows local attackers to gain root privileges on most major Linux distributions with a single command. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Linux Kernel Dirty Frag LPE Exploit Enables Root Access Across Major Distributions
item.description: Details have emerged about a new, unpatched local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability impacting the Linux kernel. Dubbed Dirty Frag, it has been described as a successor to Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, af...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Canvas login portals hacked in mass ShinyHunters extortion campaign
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang has breached education technology giant Instructure again, this time exploiting another vulnerability to deface Canvas login portals for hundreds of colleges and univer...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
